# DiamondHacks 2026

### San Diego's Largest Hackathon

**This hackathon has ended**

[Find more hackathons](https://devpost.com/hackathons) [View the winners](https://diamondhacks-2026.devpost.com/project-gallery)

###### Who can participate

* Above legal age of majority in country of residence
* College students only
* All countries/territories, excluding standard exceptions

View full rules

[View schedule](https://diamondhacks-2026.devpost.com/details/dates)

**Apr 4 – 5, 2026**

|[San Diego, CA, USA](https://maps.google.com/?q=3235%20Voigt%20Dr%2C%20La%20Jolla%2C%20CA%2092093%2C%20USA) |Public |
| --- | --- |
|**$ 25,710** in cash |**366** participants |

[ACM @ UCSD](https://devpost.com/hackathons?organization=ACM+%40+UCSD)

[Beginner Friendly](https://devpost.com/hackathons?themes%5B%5D=Beginner+Friendly) [Web](https://devpost.com/hackathons?themes%5B%5D=Web) [Machine Learning/AI](https://devpost.com/hackathons?themes%5B%5D=Machine+Learning%2FAI)
#### About the challenge

Get ready for DiamondHacks, a thrilling 36-hour hackathon tournament organized by the Association for Computing Machinery at UCSD. The sharpest minds from universities across San Diego will gather to join our magic tournament. Hundreds of hackers will team up to make projects showcasing their skills. Choose from different creative tracks and attend workshops to refine your craft. Join social events to make alliances with other talented students and celebrate magic!

Keep your wand at the ready and your eyes sharp for any tricks along the way. Let's make our mark and cast the ultimate hack!

Join us on **April 4th-5th** for an in-person competition with numerous workshops, tons of prizes, and countless opportunities for fun!

### Requirements

#### What to Submit

* Video submission: 3 minutes for video
* Submissions should be in English and uploaded on Devpost
* Explain inspirations, how the project was developed, and a short demo in the background.
* Include your code and any other other necessary files to run it in your submission
* To qualify for beginner prize, members of your team have attended at most 2 hackathons or have less than a year of programming experience

### Hackathon Sponsors

##### Partners

[](https://www.perplexity.ai/ "Go to Perplexity page") [](https://lovable.dev/ "Go to Lovable page") [](https://www.purebuttons.com/ "Go to Pure Buttons page")

### Prizes

$ 25,710 \+ in prizes

\+ other prizes

###### 1st Overall

$ 4,500 in cash

1 winner

AirPods Pro 3  
3,000 Browser Use Credits  
Browser User Merch

results.title: DiamondHacks '21: Win prizes while supporting our mission of improving gender and minority representation in tech! - Devpost
excerpts:
# DiamondHacks '21

### Win prizes while supporting our mission of improving gender and minority representation in tech!

**This hackathon has ended**

[Find more hackathons](https://devpost.com/hackathons) [View the winners](https://diamondhacks-21.devpost.com/project-gallery)

View full rules

[View schedule](https://diamondhacks-21.devpost.com/details/dates)

**Mar 27 – 28, 2021**

|Online |Public |
| --- | --- |
|**$ 4,000** in prizes |**163** participants |

[WiCS at NC State](https://devpost.com/hackathons?organization=WiCS+at+NC+State)

[Beginner Friendly](https://devpost.com/hackathons?themes%5B%5D=Beginner+Friendly) [Education](https://devpost.com/hackathons?themes%5B%5D=Education) [Social Good](https://devpost.com/hackathons?themes%5B%5D=Social+Good)

DiamondHacks '21 is on March 27-28 and is conducted with the mission of supporting women and non-binary students interested in developing software projects.
However, we welcome all hackers that support this mission, regardless of gender or experience. DiamondHacks '21 is entirely virtual and will be hosted using Hopin' and Discord.

Beginners are welcome, and will be supported with introductory workshops and guidance from fellow students. Additionally, sponsors will be available to mentor and network with students. Anyone who submits a project on DevPost will have the chance to win prizes like Amazon, Visa, Netflix, LeetCode, Uber, Spotify, and Starbucks gift cards.

Any team size is welcome, but a maximum of 4 prizes will be awarded to each winning team.

Please visit our [website](https://www.ncsudiamondhacks.com/) for more information about our schedule and logistics.

### Eligibility

Any college or high school student from any school is welcome to hack at DiamondHacks, regardless of age, experience, or gender.

There is no maximum or minimum team size. However, we have a maximum of 4 prizes to award to each winning team.
If your team is fewer than 4 people, you will only receive the number of prizes equal to the people on your team. If your team is greater than 4 people, you will receive only 4 prizes to share amongst your team. For example, a team of 3 people will receive 3 prizes and a team of 5 people will receive 4 prizes.

Teams should be made up exclusively of students who are not organizers, volunteers, judges, sponsors, or in any other privileged position at the event.

### Requirements

You are strongly encouraged to present a demo of what you have built. Pitches or presentations are discouraged. You are not judged on the quality of your pitch or the quality of your idea. As you are judged on what you built, you'll only hurt yourself by not showing a demo.

You are encouraged to present what you have done even if your hack is broken or you weren’t able to finish. It's okay if you didn't finish your hack—that happens all the time!

results.title: Diamond Hacks: Hackthon where students can compete using their coding skills and win prizes - Devpost
excerpts:
# Diamond Hacks

### Hackthon where students can compete using their coding skills and win prizes

**This hackathon has ended**

[Find more hackathons](https://devpost.com/hackathons) [View the winners](https://enrichedcs-16876.devpost.com/project-gallery)

###### Who can participate

* Ages 13 to 19 only
* All countries/territories, excluding standard exceptions

View full rules

[View schedule](https://enrichedcs-16876.devpost.com/details/dates)

**Dec 2 – 10, 2022**

|Online |Public |
| --- | --- |
|**$ 0** in prizes |**21** participants |

[non profit](https://devpost.com/hackathons?organization=non+profit)

[Beginner Friendly](https://devpost.com/hackathons?themes%5B%5D=Beginner+Friendly) [Education](https://devpost.com/hackathons?themes%5B%5D=Education) [Open Ended](https://devpost.com/hackathons?themes%5B%5D=Open+Ended)

#### About the challenge

We created Enriched CS because we fell in love with the hackathon culture.
First, collaboration in a hackathon is an amazing process, where bright minds collaborate together to create something that reflects each of their strengths. Also, every single participant holds a new learning experience.

The perhaps biggest reason for starting this hackathon is that we noticed a lot of so-called "beginner-friendly" hackathons were not really beginner friendly. Our hackathon is geared towards all levels whether you are a beginner or experienced. There will be workshops all throughout the hackathon in which you can strengthen your coding skills.

### Requirements

#### What to Build

There will be two divisions: middle school and high school. Middle schoolers will be given problems that address an issue in society. The high schoolers will be given a prompt where they have the freedom to choose how they want to create. They can use any language and IDE they would like
Note: middle schoolers **CAN** join the high school division but high schoolers **CANNOT** join the middle school division

### Hackathon Sponsors

[](https://www.taskade.com/ "Go to taskade page") [](https://www.recursivedragon.com/ "Go to Recursive Dragon page")

### Prizes

1 non-cash prize

###### $100+

1 winner

### Devpost Achievements

Submitting to this hackathon could earn you:

### Judges

**Suraj Chigati**

### Judging Criteria

* **Scoring guidline**

Questions? Email the hackathon manager

**Tell your friends**

# UC Berkeley AI Hackathon 2025

### Come join us at the world's largest AI Hackathon hosted by Cal Hacks!

**This hackathon has ended**

[Find more hackathons](https://devpost.com/hackathons) [View the winners](https://uc-berkeley-ai-hackathon-2025.devpost.com/project-gallery)

###### Who can participate

* Ages 18+ only
* Team required: 2 to 4 members
* All countries/territories, excluding standard exceptions

View full rules

[View schedule](https://uc-berkeley-ai-hackathon-2025.devpost.com/details/dates)

**Jun 21 – 22, 2025**

|[MLK Student Union](https://maps.google.com/?q=2495 Bancroft Way, Berkeley, CA 94720, USA) |Public |
| --- | --- |
|**$ 41,564** in cash |**1039** participants |

[Cal Hacks](https://devpost.com/hackathons?organization=Cal+Hacks)

[Machine Learning/AI](https://devpost.com/hackathons?themes%5B%5D=Machine+Learning%2FAI)

UC Berkeley's AI Hackathon is back! Cal Hacks will be bringing you two days of hacking on the best AI technology the world has to offer.
With support from Anthropic, Google, Fetch AI, and more, this will be a monumental event no future AI/LLM developer can afford to miss. You'll even have chance to pitch your project to investors, and win cash prizes! This event is your chance to explore the potential of large language models and other open source APIs, all while making a lasting impact in the world of artificial intelligence.

### Requirements

* Submit your hack by 6/22 11:00AM PDT and make sure to add your teammates! You'll have until 12:00PM to continue hacking and editing your submission, but the project must be submitted by 11:00AM .
* Include an image of your project and GitHub repository for any code to be considered.
* Include the table number you're presenting from and the Hacker ID of one team member in your group.
* Previous projects are not allowed.
* Your team MUST be no more than 4 team members. Solo teams are allowed.

### Hackathon Sponsors

### Prizes

\+ other prizes

results.title: Smart-Browse | Devpost
excerpts:
## Inspiration

When shopping for items, people end up using various resources like ChatGPT, Reddit, Amazon, BestBuy, Target, eBay, and other sites to figure out what is the most optimal product that fits your niche use-case. This process becomes tedious, mundane, and requires so many different tabs and spreadsheets to properly do your research before you make a purchase that really matters to you. We wanted to solve this issue by creating a dashboard that allows you to shop more smartly using AI.

## What it does

The system first takes in a prompt for what kind of item you want to look for. Let us say you are shopping for a speaker.

Based on this, it searches through Reddit and its general knowledge base to figure out a set of dynamic questions to ask you about the specifics for that item. Continuing on the Speaker example, let us say an example question is "Where would you see yourself using this speaker?".

...

We used various different webscrapers, API calling functions, a Python Flask server, MongoDB for authentication and saving public/private lists, ElevenLabs's Text-to-Speech feature, and dynamic UI to allow the agent to control different parts of the UI to prompt the user in interesting means while maintaining simplicity.

## Challenges we ran into

Some challenges we faced along the way were scraping data from websites like Amazon or eBay to be able to get images, prices, reviews, and other information that we can then use to display within our website. By using different technologies like Playwright, we were able to get the information we wanted and provide our users with the full experience.

Another challenge we faced was ...

## Accomplishments that we're proud of

We are proud of building a user experience that chatbots like ChatGPT or Gemini cannot provide on their websites.

...
